lines.text: Fourth of July stock. Bought it two years before they banned the good ones. [pause] Nobody wrote it down anyplace.
[flatly] For what.

I did two years on a missile submarine off Alaska, listening for Soviet propellers, so a man like Earl could keep a barn out here no Russian could touch. [angry] And they send a fella in a zip-up vest with a sticker gun.
(then) Commies. [flatly] I don't care what flag they fly over the building.
These'll go four, five hundred feet, [chuckles] easy.
[flatly] Three hundred. It's printed on the box.
They always look higher than that once it's good and dark.
I hauled that trainer up from Houston in a horse trailer. Seventy-five. [pause] Signed it off a supply captain who wanted it gone off his books.
(then) Fifty years I kept the government's property safe for the government. [pause] And they sent a man to take it back like I stole it.
Office locks up at five. That gravel lot out back is empty by dark. [pause] Four cases of these up over the parking lot, and we're home before the news.
[flatly] That's a federal building.
It's a strip mall with a flagpole, Earl.
I've got a grandson who still thinks I hung the moon. [pause] I'm not explaining a courtroom to him. Count me out.
Rest of us, then. Friday.
Do what you're going to do. [pause] Just don't tell me about it after.
Y'all want to tell me whose idea this was, [pause] or should I guess?
Mine.
His, but I brought the truck.
I just held the lighter. [flatly] Following orders.
Anything left in that pipe?
[flatly] She's cold.
How do you gentlemen plead.
Guilty.
Guilty on all of it.
Guilty, Your Honor.

I was nine years old in my daddy's living room the night men from this state walked on the Moon. [pause] Watched it on a television the size of a bread box. Time served. Four hundred dollars each, plus the cost of the gravel. [pause] I've got real criminals on this morning's docket, gentlemen. Get on home.
(to Cricket) [sarcastic] Come to gloat?
[warmly] Came to drive you home.
You could've come.
[quietly] I know it.
